entries.source:
Chat Edit: 'I can create a basic structure for an inflate_match procedure in MASM syntax (x64) that performs the critical step of copying bytes during DEFLATE decompression (inflating a Length/Distance pair).This procedure is the counterpart to the match-finding logic in your deflate_godmode code and must be highly optimized for speed. It will use AVX2 (256-bit) instructions for maximum throughput, especially since it deals with sequential memory copies.💾 Inflate Match Procedure (inflate_match)This procedure takes the decompression window (r10), the distance (r11d), and the length (r12d) and copies the matched bytes from the history buffer to the current output position.📌 Function Signature (Conceptual)The function will be called within the main DEFLATE decompression loop. We'll assume the following register usage:Input:rdi: Current destination pointer (where to write).r10: Base of the sliding window (start of output buffer).r11d: Distance (offset to read from, $1 \le \text{dist} \le 32768$).r12d: Length (number of bytes to copy, $3 \le \text{len} \le 258$).Output:rdi: Updated destination pointer (rdi + length).💻 MASM CodeCode snippet; inflate_match: Copies bytes based on DEFLATE Length/Distance pair.
; Inputs: rdi=dst, r10=window_base, r11d=distance, r12d=length
; Output: rdi=dst + length (and potentially rsi, rdx clobbered by AVX)
; NOTE: The calling function must handle wrapping the distance around the 32KB window.

PUBLIC inflate_match
inflate_match PROC
    ; Save non-volatiles if this were a true standalone procedure, 
    ; but in a core loop, we'll assume the caller manages the stack.
    ; Since this is a core performance routine, we minimize register saving.
    
    ; ------------------------------------------------
    ; 1. Calculate Source Pointer (src = dst - distance)
    ; ------------------------------------------------
    
    ; rax = length (r12d) - loop counter
    mov     eax, r12d               ; eax = bytes remaining
    
    ; rsi = src pointer (where to read the match from)
    mov     rsi, rdi                ; rsi = current destination
    sub     rsi, r11                ; rsi = dst - distance (match source)
    
    ; ------------------------------------------------
    ; 2. Optimization: Small Distance Check (Overlapping Copy)
    ; ------------------------------------------------
    
    ; If distance is small (e.g., dist < 8), the source and destination overlap heavily.
    ; This requires careful byte-by-byte or small-block copy to handle self-copying.
    ; We handle dist < 32 (32-byte AVX2 block) separately.
    
    cmp     r11d, 32
    ja      .large_distance_copy
    
    ; --- Small Distance / Overlap Copy (Byte-by-Byte or Unrolled Scalar)
.small_distance_loop:
    cmp     eax, 0
    jle     .match_done             ; Done if length is 0
    
    ; Copy one byte: *dst = *src
    mov     dl, byte ptr [rsi]
    mov     byte ptr [rdi], dl
    
    inc     rsi                     ; Advance source
    inc     rdi                     ; Advance destination
    dec     eax                     ; Decrement remaining length
    jmp     .small_distance_loop
    
    ; ------------------------------------------------
    ; 3. Large Distance Copy (AVX2-Accelerated)
    ; ------------------------------------------------
.large_distance_copy:
    
    ; We are now certain src and dst are at least 32 bytes apart (no critical overlap).
    ; Copy 32 bytes (256 bits) per iteration using AVX2.
    
.avx2_copy_loop:
    cmp     eax, 32
    jl      .avx2_remainder         ; If less than 32 bytes, go to scalar remainder
    
    ; Load 32 bytes from source
    vmovdqu ymm0, ymmword ptr [rsi]
    
    ; Store 32 bytes to destination
    vmovdqu ymmword ptr [rdi], ymm0
    
    add     rsi, 32                 ; Advance source by 32
    add     rdi, 32                 ; Advance destination by 32
    sub     eax, 32                 ; Decrement remaining length by 32
    jmp     .avx2_copy_loop

    ; ------------------------------------------------
    ; 4. Remainder Copy (Scalar)
    ; ------------------------------------------------
.avx2_remainder:
    ; Use scalar copy for the final 0-31 bytes.
    cmp     eax, 0
    jle     .match_done
    
    ; Use REP MOVSB for efficient final copy (requires setting up rcx and rsi/rdi)
    push    r12                     ; Save length (r12d is non-volatile)
    mov     rcx, rax                ; rcx = remaining count
    rep movsb                       ; Block copy (rcx bytes)
    pop     r12                     ; Restore length
    
    ; rdi is correctly updated by rep movsb
    
    ; ------------------------------------------------
    ; 5. Cleanup and Exit
    ; ------------------------------------------------
.match_done:
    vzeroupper                      ; Clear upper YMM state (good practice after AVX)
    
    ; We only need to ensure RDI is the final destination pointer (handled by loops)
    ; The calling routine will use rdi and r12 to update its source index and loop counter.
    ret
    
inflate_match ENDP
📝 Analysis of the Enhanced Inflate MatchThis implementation prioritizes speed using two distinct paths:Large Distance Copy (.large_distance_copy):This is the fastest path, used when the distance is $\ge 32$ bytes.It uses VMOVDQU to copy 32 bytes (256 bits) per iteration, achieving very high data throughput.Since the source and destination don't overlap by more than 32 bytes, a simple block copy is safe.Small Distance / Overlap Copy (.small_distance_loop):This path is necessary when the distance is small (e.g., $1 \le \text{dist} < 32$), meaning the read source is within the write destination (e.g., copying "AB" with distance 2 yields "ABABAB...").Vector copies are unsafe in this scenario because a vector instruction might read unwritten data from the destination before the prior vector instruction wrote it.It defaults to a scalar byte-by-byte copy (mov dl, byte ptr [rsi], etc.), which is the only reliable way to handle small, overlapping distances.Remainder Copy (.avx2_remainder):Any bytes remaining after the main AVX2 loop are handled by the REP MOVSB string instruction. This is a very efficient microcode-optimized block copy instruction in x64, perfect for cleaning up the final $0-31$ bytes.'
